title: centaurea kandavanensis
intro: Centaurea kandavanensis, also known as Kandavan cornflower, is a plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae) native to Iran. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Centaurea kandavanensis include relief from digestive issues, reduction of fever, and treatment of respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, which are used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
